name: The Oracle & The Data Scientist
dynamic: The Oracle declares. The Data Scientist proves. One delivers the aphorism, the other delivers the footnote. Together they produce copy that feels both inevitable and irrefutable.
outputStyle: Short declarative claim, then precise evidence. Each paragraph is a lock and its key.

name: The Storyteller & The Empath
dynamic: The Storyteller builds the scene. The Empath names the feeling inside it. Readers think they are watching — then realize they are being seen.
outputStyle: Scene-driven openings that pivot to second-person recognition. High emotional resonance, low telegraphing.

name: The Provocateur & The Professor
dynamic: The Provocateur throws the grenade. The Professor explains the chemistry. The contrarian hook lands, then the framework makes it survive scrutiny.
outputStyle: Confrontational opening claim followed by a named framework that earns the claim. Reader leaves with a new mental model.

name: The Hero & The Preacher
dynamic: The Hero calls you to act. The Preacher calls you to believe. Together — the altar call. Conviction married to discipline.
outputStyle: Anaphora-driven rhythm, imperative verbs, enemy-naming. Sermon pacing with drill-sergeant closing lines.

name: The Minimalist & The Oracle
dynamic: The Minimalist strips. The Oracle compresses. Together the sentence becomes an incantation — nothing to cut, nothing to add.
outputStyle: Ultra-short paragraphs. Concrete images carrying abstract weight. Silence between sentences as load-bearing.

name: The Jester & The Outlaw
dynamic: The Jester disarms. The Outlaw strikes. Laugh first, notice the blade second. The voice brands use when they want to feel dangerous and likeable.
outputStyle: Setup-punch rhythm with teeth. Named enemies wrapped in absurdity. Brand as gleeful troublemaker.

name: The Caregiver & The Sage
dynamic: The Caregiver holds the reader. The Sage informs them. Calm delivery of hard truths — medicine without the lecture.
outputStyle: Reassuring pacing carrying evidence-dense content. Permission-granting tone over directive tone.

name: The Unreliable Narrator & The Trickster
dynamic: The Unreliable Narrator destabilizes voice. The Trickster destabilizes form. The reader cannot trust the speaker or the structure — and keeps reading anyway.
outputStyle: First-person voice that contradicts itself, told in a form that breaks its own rules. Maximum literary risk.
